perturbation.original_text: This Agreement shall commence on the Effective Date and shall continue until the four-year anniversary of the Effective Date (such period, as it may be extended, either by the mutual written agreement of the parties or automatically, or earlier terminated being referred to as the "Consultation Period"), unless sooner terminated in accordance with the provisions of Section 4, and shall automatically renew for successive one-year periods, unless the Company provides 90 days' notice of termination before any such successive period.
perturbation.changed_text: This Agreement shall commence on the Effective Date and shall continue until the four-year anniversary of the Effective Date (such period, as it may be extended, either by the mutual written agreement of the parties or automatically, or earlier terminated being referred to as the "Consultation Period"), unless sooner terminated in accordance with the provisions of Section 4, and will require mutual agreement to renew for successive one-year periods, unless the Company provides 90 days' notice of termination before any such successive period.
perturbation.explanation: The original text states that the contract auto-renews unless terminated by the company. The changed text requires 'mutual agreement' to renew, which contradicts the original automatic renewal clause. This introduces uncertainty about whether renewal is automatic or requires both parties' consent.
perturbation.contradicted_text: This Agreement shall commence on the Effective Date and shall continue until the four-year anniversary of the Effective Date (such period, as it may be extended, either by the mutual written agreement of the parties or automatically, or earlier terminated being referred to as the "Consultation Period"), unless sooner terminated in accordance with the provisions of Section 4, and will require mutual agreement to renew for successive one-year periods, unless the Company provides 90 days' notice of termination before any such successive period.
perturbation.justification: The original text specifies automatic renewal, while the changed text requires 'mutual agreement' for renewal. This creates a direct conflict regarding the renewal process, as the contradicted text ('This Agreement shall commence on the Effective Date and shall continue until the four-year anniversary of the Effective Date (such period, as it may be extended, either by the mutual written agreement of the parties or automatically, or earlier terminated being referred to as the 'Consultation Period'), unless sooner terminated in accordance with the provisions of Section 4, and will require mutual agreement to renew for successive one-year periods, unless the Company provides 90 days' notice of termination before any such successive period.') now contains contradictory statements. This falls under the definition of Structural Flaws - In Text Contradiction, where a clause is placed in multiple locations with conflicting implications, creating uncertainty about the obligations and rights of each party.

perturbation.original_text: The Company shall reimburse the Consultant for all reasonable and necessary documented out of pocket expenses incurred or paid by the Consultant in connection with, or related to, the performance of Consultant's services under this Agreement.
perturbation.changed_text: The Company may reimburse the Consultant for reasonable out of pocket expenses incurred or paid by the Consultant in connection with, or related to, the performance of Consultant's services under this Agreement, but reimbursement will depend on budget constraints.
perturbation.explanation: The original text states that the Company 'shall' reimburse the Consultant for all reasonable expenses. The changed text makes reimbursement discretionary ('may') and dependent on 'budget constraints', which contradicts the original mandatory reimbursement clause. Now, the Company has the right to deny reimbursement if budget is limited. This creates financial uncertainty.
perturbation.contradicted_text: Expenses. The Company may reimburse the Consultant for reasonable out of pocket expenses incurred or paid by the Consultant in connection with, or related to, the performance of Consultant's services under this Agreement, but reimbursement will depend on budget constraints. The Consultant shall submit to the Company itemized monthly statements, in a form satisfactory to the Company, of such expenses incurred in the previous month. The Company shall pay to the Consultant amounts shown on each such statement within thirty (30) days after receipt thereof.
perturbation.justification: The original text guarantees reimbursement ('shall'), while the changed text makes it discretionary and budget-dependent ('may'). The contradicted text includes the modified text: 'The Company may reimburse the Consultant for reasonable out of pocket expenses incurred or paid by the Consultant in connection with, or related to, the performance of Consultant's services under this Agreement, but reimbursement will depend on budget constraints.' and then proceeds to say 'The Company shall pay to the Consultant amounts shown on each such statement within thirty (30) days after receipt thereof.' This creates a direct conflict. The shall pay conflicts with the budget constraint text, because it makes it seem like payment is guaranteed after the consultant submits an expense report, despite budget limits. This qualifies as a Structural Flaws - In Text Contradiction because the clause is placed in multiple locations with conflicting implications, creating uncertainty about the obligations and rights of each party.
